text:
One of the Olympians competing in the triathlon atParis2024 couldn’t quite stomach swimming in the River Seine.

The triathlon has been one of the most talked about sports at this summer’s games due to concerns over athletes swimming in the Seine.

Organisers had spent over a £1billiontrying to clean up the river in time, yet the event was postponed from Tuesday morning after tests showedpollution levels were still too high.

The green light was given on Wednesday morning for both the women’s and men’s races to take place, with Team GB enjoying success in both.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Beth Potter won bronze beforeAlex Yee pulling off an epic late comebackto win Great Britain’s fifth gold of Paris.

Not everyone had a pleasant experience however, namely Canada’s Tyler Mislawchuk who crossed the line a very credible ninth, some 52 seconds after Yee.

Le#TriathlonÃ§a demande de sacrÃ©s efforts ðð»pic.twitter.com/0Ajve53sxr

Like many, the 29-year-old looked worse for wear as he arrived at the finish and after stumbling past Yee and the other medalists, cameras caught him throwing up in a sight not for the squeamish.

It may have just been a natural reaction with the triathlon one of the most gruelling sports at theOlympics, but the image of Mislawchuk hurling won’t help concerns over the threat of E.coli.

There is still the mixed triathlon relay to come on August 5, as well as the marathon swimming which is also taking place in the Seine.

Some athletes have even resorted to desperate measures in order to fend off any potential health hazards, with the USA’s Seth Rider revealing he wasnot washing his handsin order to build-up a tolerance to bacteria.

‘In preparation for this race, I knew there was going to be some E.coli exposure. So I’ve been trying to increase my E.coli threshold by exposing myself to a bit of E.coli in day-to-day life.’

Asked how he’s exposed himself to more bacteria, Rider added: ‘It’s a proven method, backed by science.

‘It’s just little things, throughout your day. Like not washing your hands after you go to the bathroom and stuff like that.’

The strategy may have prevented illness but it did not improve performance, with Rider finishing 29th out of 55 in Wednesday’s race.

title: Olympic triathlete throws up after swimming in filthy River Seine | Metro News
description: One of the Olympians competing in the triathlon at the Paris 2024 Olympics couldn't quite stomach swimming in the River Seine.
